summary: Canvas-based 9×9 Go board with alternating black/white play and solid controls, but capture logic is undemonstrated and the top-left corner intersection is never clickable.
evidence_highlights: Turn alternation confirmed across all scenarios: black stones (brightness ~14–86) and white stones (~190–197) place in strict alternation, and turn is retained after the rejected P00 click
Center hoshi at P44 clearly present from the first frame (brightness 39 vs. board ~155–166) and stable throughout every scenario
Four controls rendered and functional: Pass, Undo, New Game, Score; per-player capture counters displayed
evidence_gaps: P00 (top-left corner intersection) produces 0 changed pixels on every click in every scenario — consistent click-detection failure, not occupied-point rejection
Capture sequence with white group at P11/P21/P12 shows Black Captures: 0 throughout; no capture evidence anywhere in the evidence
Corner hoshi positions P22, P26, P62, P66 show only 1 brightness-unit contrast against neighbors — no convincing hoshi dots beyond the tengen
No evidence of suicide prevention or ko detection
